paragraphs.body: I remember about a year ago I was getting treatment at my old club in Germany and some of my teammates were in the training room as well just fucking around and having fun, my athletic trainer, who was German just like we all were, turned to me and asked me: “ Do you understand what they are talking about right now?” And of course, I knew what every word meant that they were saying and to be honest in that moment I didn’t think much about it. But when we started talking about languages in this class I somehow got reminded of that moment and it just got me thinking in a whole different way about "Languages". There aren't only languages spoken by certain countries like German, English, or Chinese. There are also different languages within these languages. This might be the way that you talk to your parents or the way the way you talk to your friends. You probably even use different languages for different friend groups. But all of them have one thing in common: They make you feel some kind of way. And most of the time that way is being comfortable.

annotations.text: There aren't only languages spoken by certain countries like German, English, or Chinese. There are also different languages within these languages. This might be the way that you talk to your parents or the way the way you talk to your friends. You probably even use different languages for different friend groups. But all of them have one thing in common: They make you feel some kind of way. And most of the time that way is being comfortable.

paragraphs.body: To me, language is one of the most important things. And when I am saying that I am not just talking about these personal languages we wrote about in the previous assignments. Language, as in different languages that are being spoken in the world, is so valuable. I feel like after my 12 years of school that I've had so far I learned a lot of different stuff. To be honest, about 85% of that I will never need again in my life, but what has really helped me, especially coming to the U.S. and into a team with people from about 10 different countries, is the 3 different languages that I learned throughout my time in school. That is also something that will make finding a job much easier for you. Many companies are looking for people who can communicate in different languages. On the other hand, when talking about the languages I wrote about in previous assignments, these languages are things that make you feel comfortable or feel at home. For example, the "home" language that you speak to your family is just something that you need in your life to feel comfortable. It is also something that educates you. For example the saying "Hochmut kommt vor dem Fall” that You usually say to people that are being very cocky or arrogant, which you shouldn't be because if you are being arrogant, that will get you the downfall. My parents always used to tell me this saying when I was younger after I was saying cocky or arrogant things, which is used to do very often. I used to think "What are they talking about? I am not even arrogant", but now thinking back at it, I think I was. And that helped me, like many other things my parents taught me, to get to the place I am now. Because now I feel like I am very humble while still being confident I just keep that part more to myself. And today I think you aren't going to get anywhere in life if you are being too arrogant. So, as you can see language also helped me in that aspect too. Another aspect of language is, coming back to the Introduction, Language is also something that separates people. That might sound bad at first because we nowadays often get to hear that everything is about unity and staying together, which I completely agree with. But sometimes you and your friends for example just want to be separated from for example your parents or other adults. Like I described in the Introduction sometimes me and my teammates don’t really want our athletic trainer to understand what we are talking about, whether that may be because we want some secrets not to get out or we just want to feel a feeling of community. Language is a thing that can create that.

annotations.text: And today I think you aren't going to get anywhere in life if you are being too arrogant.
annotations.comment: This is an interesting observation, but I'm not sure how it's connected to the value of learning multiple languages?

annotations.text: especially coming to the U.S. and into a team with people from about 10 different countries, is the 3 different languages that I learned throughout my time in school.
annotations.comment: interesting! Why did you learn three?

annotations.text: And when I am saying that I am not just talking about these personal languages we wrote about in the previous assignments.
But sometimes you and your friends for example just want to be separated from for example your parents or other adults.
annotations.comment: Another new and different idea. Let's talk about what's really at the heart of this essay!

paragraphs.body: Living in Germany means knowing For winter you will need a winter coat And every time of the year raindrops will be falling And in summer you can ship all the lakes with a boat Living in Germany means knowing That as a boy you will have to try soccer And if you are not going to be balling Then you are at least going to be a supporter Languages are just a thing that is very diverse and has so many different positive aspects. I didn't notice that until I really thought about it in this class and got to notice the different languages that you speak throughout the day depending on who you are talking to. You adapt your language to almost everyone that you are talking to and the people to where you adapt your language the least, for me personally I would say those are my parents, my girlfriend, and my closest friends, these are the people you are most comfortable around and there are always phrases in every one of those groups that bring back memories and make you feel comfortable and kind of “home”.

annotations.text: And if you are not going to be balling

annotations.comment: These are lovely details, but how does this poem support your essay's central focusing idea? What is your essay really about?
annotations.context_right: For winter you will need a winter coat

paragraphs.body: Language is incredibly diverse and has many positive aspects that often go unnoticed until we reflect on how we use it in daily life. I didn’t fully realize this until I took this class and started to notice the different ways I adapt my language based on who I’m speaking to. We naturally adjust our language to fit the context of different conversations, whether it's with friends, family, or acquaintances. For me personally, the people where I adapt my language the least are my parents, my girlfriend, and my closest friends. These are the people I feel most comfortable around, and with them, my language feels most natural and effortless.

annotations.comment: You're repeating your ideas in these two paragraphs, though--mostly--the ideas are still a bit vague. Let's talk about the audience, purpose, and "so what" of this essay :)
annotations.context_right: Language is incredibly diverse and has many positive aspects that often go unnotice

paragraphs.body: There are always phrases in every one of those groups that bring back memories and make you feel comfortable and kind of "home". The familiarity of certain words or ways of speaking with these close connections can bring back feelings of comfort. It’s almost like being “home” in the sense that the language used within these groups carries a deeper emotional meaning. This realization has made me appreciate that language is not only a tool for communication but also plays a significant role in shaping and reinforcing the bonds we share with those we care about. Language, in this way, becomes more than just words—it becomes a part of the emotions that connect us to others and shape our closest relationships.

annotations.comment: Don't forget to "echo" your intro in your conclusion :)
annotations.context_left: ds—it becomes a part of the emotions that connect us to others and shape our closest relationships.
